title: sparaxis variegata subsp. metelerkampiae
intro: Sparaxis variegata subsp. metelerkampiae is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the "Dainty Sabella" or "Pincushion Flower". The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in South Africa, where it is believed to possess medicinal properties that can be used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Sparaxis variegata subsp. metelerkampiae include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, and are also believed to have antiseptic and antiviral properties.
